agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Parcel delivery logistics platform managing shipment lifecycle from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers. This is a core bounded context governing shipment state management and billing classification.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A domain entity representing a parcel moving from a pickup address to a delivery address, with a defined weight and lifecycle state."},{"term":"Pickup Address","definition":"The origin location where a parcel is collected. Required at shipment creation."},{"term":"Delivery Address","definition":"The destination location where a parcel is to be delivered. Required at shipment creation."},{"term":"Shipment State","definition":"The lifecycle phase of a shipment. Valid states: Pending, In Transit, Delivered. Transitions are strictly forward-only."},{"term":"Pending","definition":"Initial state of a shipment after creation, before carrier pickup."},{"term":"In Transit","definition":"State indicating the shipment has been picked up and is en route. Irreversible — cannot return to Pending."},{"term":"Delivered","definition":"Terminal state indicating the parcel has reached its delivery address. No further state transitions allowed."},{"term":"Weight","definition":"The physical mass of the parcel in kilograms. Must be positive and at most 1000 kg."},{"term":"Weight Bracket","definition":"A pricing classification derived from shipment weight: Standard (0–10 kg], Heavy (10–50 kg], Freight (>50 kg)."},{"term":"Standard","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 0 kg up to and including 10 kg."},{"term":"Heavy","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 10 kg up to and including 50 kg."},{"term":"Freight","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 50 kg up to and including 1000 kg."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A shipment requires both a pickup address and a delivery address at creation time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment without origin and destination is physically meaningless — it cannot exist in any valid state. This is a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create a shipment missing either pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Shipment state transitions are strictly forward-only: Pending → In Transit → Delivered. No backward transitions are permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Reversing a physical logistics state (e.g., un-shipping a parcel) would corrupt the audit trail and violate the real-world process. State integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"A shipment in state 'In Transit' cannot transition to 'Pending'. A shipment in state 'Delivered' cannot transition to any other state."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Delivered is a terminal state — no further state changes are allowed on a delivered shipment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Once a parcel is delivered, the physical handoff is complete. Changing state would corrupt downstream billing, tracking, and proof-of-delivery records.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to change the state of a Delivered shipment must be rejected regardless of the target state."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment weight must be strictly greater than 0 kg and must not exceed 1000 kg.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Zero or negative weight is physically impossible for a real parcel. The 1000 kg upper bound is a physical/operational constraint (vehicle limits, safety). Violating this corrupts billing and operational planning.","testable_assertion":"Creating or updating a shipment with weight ≤ 0 or weight > 1000 must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Weight bracket is determined by weight: Standard for (0, 10] kg, Heavy for (10, 50] kg, Freight for (50, 1000] kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The bracket thresholds and names are business policy — a VP could adjust the ranges (e.g., raise Standard to 15 kg) or add new tiers without corrupting system integrity.","testable_assertion":"A 10 kg shipment is classified as Standard. A 10.01 kg shipment is classified as Heavy. A 50 kg shipment is classified as Heavy. A 50.01 kg shipment is classified as Freight."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A request to create a shipment with a valid pickup address, delivery address, and weight of 5 kg","when":"The shipment is created","then":"The shipment is persisted in Pending state with weight bracket Standard"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A request to create a shipment missing a pickup address","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating pickup address is required"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A request to create a shipment missing a delivery address","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating delivery address is required"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"It transitions to In Transit","then":"The state is updated to In Transit and the transition is recorded"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A shipment in In Transit state","when":"An attempt is made to transition it back to Pending","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains In Transit"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A shipment in In Transit state","when":"It transitions to Delivered","then":"The state is updated to Delivered and the transition is recorded as final"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A shipment in Delivered state","when":"Any state change is attempted (to Pending, In Transit, or Delivered)","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains Delivered"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A request to create a shipment with weight of 0 kg","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating weight must be positive"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A request to create a shipment with weight of -5 kg","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating weight must be positive"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A request to create a shipment with weight of 1001 kg","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating weight must not exceed 1000 kg"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A shipment with weight of exactly 10 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The shipment is classified as Standard"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A shipment with weight of 10.01 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The shipment is classified as Heavy"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A shipment with weight of exactly 50 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The shipment is classified as Heavy"},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A shipment with weight of 50.01 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The shipment is classified as Freight"},{"id":"AC-15","given":"A shipment with weight of exactly 1000 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The shipment is classified as Freight"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Weight at exact boundary of 10 kg","expected_behavior":"Classified as Standard (upper bound inclusive for the lower bracket)"},{"scenario":"Weight at exact boundary of 50 kg","expected_behavior":"Classified as Heavy (upper bound inclusive for the lower bracket)"},{"scenario":"Weight at exact maximum of 1000 kg","expected_behavior":"Accepted and classified as Freight"},{"scenario":"Weight of 0.001 kg (near-zero but positive)","expected_behavior":"Accepted and classified as Standard — the spec says positive, not a practical minimum"},{"scenario":"Shipment created in Pending then immediately transitioned to Delivered, skipping In Transit","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — spec implies linear progression but does not explicitly forbid skipping. See escalation."},{"scenario":"Attempt to re-deliver an already Delivered shipment (Delivered → Delivered)","expected_behavior":"Rejected — Delivered is terminal, no state changes of any kind are permitted"},{"scenario":"Addresses provided but empty strings","expected_behavior":"Should be rejected — an empty string is not a valid address, though spec does not define address validation rules"},{"scenario":"Weight provided as non-numeric or null","expected_behavior":"Rejected at validation layer — weight must be a positive number"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Weight bracket misclassification directly impacts revenue. Incorrect boundaries (e.g., classifying a 50.5 kg shipment as Heavy instead of Freight) means under-charging. Correct bracket enforcement protects billing integrity.","operational":"Forward-only state machine prevents operational confusion — dispatchers, warehouse staff, and drivers all depend on accurate shipment state. Allowing backward transitions would break routing, scanning workflows, and SLA tracking.","user":"Customers expect shipment tracking to reflect reality. A delivered parcel reverting to in-transit destroys trust. Clear weight limits set expectations at booking time, preventing failed pickups for overweight parcels.","risk":"Primary risk is bracket boundary ambiguity at exactly 10 kg and 50 kg — if implementations disagree on inclusive vs exclusive bounds, billing disputes will arise. Secondary risk is the undefined Pending → Delivered skip path, which could be exploited or cause audit gaps.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Shippers (customers)","impact":"Weight limits and bracket pricing directly affect their costs. Clear state tracking affects their visibility and trust."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"State machine integrity is critical for routing and handoff workflows. Backward transitions would break dispatch planning."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Weight brackets drive invoicing. Boundary errors cause revenue leakage or customer disputes."},{"group":"Drivers / Couriers","impact":"State transitions trigger their pickup and delivery workflows. Corrupt state means wasted trips."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Terminal Delivered state eliminates ambiguity in dispute resolution. Clear rules reduce support ticket complexity."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is reasonably clear on core rules — state machine, weight constraints, and bracket definitions. Confidence is docked because: (1) bracket boundary inclusivity is inferred, not stated explicitly (the PM said '0-10', '10-50', 'over 50' which leaves the exact-boundary behavior ambiguous); (2) whether Pending → Delivered skip is allowed is unstated; (3) address validation depth is undefined; (4) no mention of whether weight can be modified after creation.","high_confidence_areas":["Forward-only state machine with Delivered as terminal","Weight must be positive and ≤ 1000 kg","Three weight brackets exist: Standard, Heavy, Freight","Both addresses required at creation"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact boundary inclusivity for weight brackets (is 10 kg Standard or Heavy?)","Whether Pending → Delivered direct transition is allowed","Whether weight or addresses can be modified after shipment creation","Address validation rules beyond presence check"],"assumptions":["Weight bracket boundaries are upper-bound inclusive: (0,10] = Standard, (10,50] = Heavy, (50,1000] = Freight — this is the most common logistics convention","Pending → Delivered without passing through In Transit is not allowed, as the spec implies a linear progression","Weight is immutable after shipment creation — the spec only mentions it at creation time","Addresses are non-empty strings; no further format validation is specified"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The PM's bracket description uses '0-10 kg', '10-50 kg', and 'over 50 kg'. It is unclear whether the boundary values (exactly 10 kg, exactly 50 kg) belong to the lower or upper bracket. Convention suggests upper-bound inclusive for the lower bracket, but this must be confirmed to prevent billing disputes.","affected_stakeholders":["Finance / Billing","Shippers","Customer Support"],"options":["A: Boundary-inclusive lower bracket — 10 kg is Standard, 50 kg is Heavy","B: Boundary-inclusive upper bracket — 10 kg is Heavy, 50 kg is Freight"],"recommendation":"Option A (lower bracket owns the boundary). This is standard logistics convention and more customer-friendly (cheaper tier at boundary)."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Can a shipment transition directly from Pending to Delivered, skipping In Transit? The spec says 'once in transit it cannot go back to pending' and 'delivered is final', but does not address the skip scenario. This matters for edge cases like same-location deliveries or manual corrections.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations / Dispatch","Drivers / Couriers","Finance / Billing"],"options":["A: Strict linear — must pass through In Transit before Delivered","B: Allow skip — Pending can go directly to Delivered"],"recommendation":"Option A (strict linear). In logistics, the In Transit scan is an operational checkpoint that triggers billing, driver assignment, and tracking events. Skipping it creates audit gaps."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No rules provided for whether shipment weight or addresses can be modified after creation. In real logistics, weight corrections happen frequently (e.g., after physical weighing at depot). This affects billing bracket recalculation and audit trails.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations / Dispatch","Finance / Billing","Shippers"],"options":["A: Immutable after creation — no changes allowed","B: Mutable only in Pending state","C: Mutable with audit trail in any non-terminal state"],"recommendation":"Option B (mutable only in Pending). Once in transit, weight is physically verified and locked for billing. But this needs PM confirmation."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
